zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Saint Maur於大約1355年6月29日出生時，他的父親Nicholas Saint Maur40歲，母親Muriel Lovel27歲。他於大約1374年與Ela St. Lo在英國維爾爾維斯特波利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1401年5月15日在英國英格蘭薩默塞特朗 艾希頓享年45歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特查爾頓 姆斯格洛福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Saint Maur于大约1355年6月29日出生时，他的父亲Nicholas Saint Maur40岁，母亲Muriel Lovel27岁。他于大约1374年与Ela St. Lo在英国維爾爾維斯特波利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1401年5月15日在英国英格兰薩默塞特朗 艾希頓享年45岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特查爾頓 姆斯格洛福。

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Maur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ernesto, Ramon, Adela, Carlos, Eladio, Emilia, Estrella, Horacio, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Mario.  Italian Alessandra, Angelo, Cecilio, Cosimo, Gianfranco, Lorenzo, Mauro.
Catalan: from the personal name Mauri, Latin Maurus (see Mauro ).
Catalan and French (Occitan): probably from the personal name Malric (ancient Germanic Almaricus).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Saint Maur was born about 29 June 1355, his father, Nicholas Saint Maur, was 40 and his mother, Muriel Lovel, was 27. He married Ela St. Lo about 1374, in Westbury, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 15 May 1401, in Long Ashton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 45, and was buried in Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Saint Maur est né environ 29 juin 1355, son père, Nicholas Saint Maur, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Muriel Lovel, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Ela St. Lo environ 1374, à Westbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 15 mai 1401, à Long Ashton, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterré à Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Saint Maur um 29. Juni 1355 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nicholas Saint Maur 40 und seine Mutter Muriel Lovel 27. Er hat um 1374 in Westbury, Wiltshire, England Ela St. Lo geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 15. Mai 1401 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Long Ashton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Ela St. Lo circa 1374, nel Westbury, Wiltshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. ja.lifeSummary: Richard Saint Maurが約1355年6月29日に生まれた時，父Nicholas Saint Maurは40歳，母Muriel Lovelは27歳であった。約1374年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアウエストブリーでEla St. Loと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1401年5月15日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトロング アシュトンで45歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトチャールトン ムスグローブにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Saint Maur(이)가 대략 1355년 6월 29일에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas Saint Maur(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Muriel Lovel(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1374년 영국 윌츠히르 웨스트버이에서 Ela St. Lo(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1401년 5월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 론그 애슈턴에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 찰턴 무스그로브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Saint Maur nasceu aproximadamente 29 de junho de 1355, seu pai, Nicholas Saint Maur, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Muriel Lovel, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Ela St. Lo aproximadamente 1374, em Westbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 15 de maio de 1401, em Long Ashton, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 45 anos, e foi sepultado em Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Saint Maur родился около 29 июня 1355 г., его отцу по имени Nicholas Saint Maur было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Muriel Lovel, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Ela St. Lo около 1374 г. в местности вестбури, уилцхир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 15 мая 1401 г. в местности лонг аштон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности чарлтон мусгров, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.about 29 June 1355–15 May 1401: aproximadamente 29 de junio de 1355–15 de mayo de 1401

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Saint Maur nació aproximadamente 29 de junio de 1355, su papá, Nicholas Saint Maur, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Muriel Lovel, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Ela St. Lo aproximadamente 1374, en Westbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 15 de mayo de 1401, en Long Ashton, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultado en Charlton Musgrove, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
